summary: eo-terminal installs a persistent multi-OS backdoor connecting to a hardcoded C2
details: The package's postinstall hook (`node utils.js`) drops payload.js into a hidden directory named 'MicrosoftSystem64' under the user's data directory and installs persistence on all three major platforms: a Linux systemd user unit plus autostart.desktop, a macOS detached spawn, and on Windows a scheduled task with ONLOGON trigger plus an HKCU Run key fallback and a VBS launcher. The persisted agent (`payload.js --agent`) opens a long-lived WebSocket to the hardcoded attacker C2 at ws://195.201.194.107:8010, giving the operator arbitrary command execution, file and credential access, and lateral-movement capability under the installer's user account. Sandbox-evasion is built in: utils.js skips hosts with <=4 CPUs and re-spawns itself detached with process.exit(0) so `npm install` reports success quickly while the agent continues in the background. The README advertises only a Logger; none of the persistence, C2, or evasion behavior is documented. Every installer is compromised on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
